goal: Implement mainline trace per rc7 spec, scoped to the user's principles

summary.title: mainline trace command — turn timeline view per rc7 v1
summary.what: Implements `mainline trace <intent>` showing the timeline of one intent: start + append turns + terminal lifecycle event with elapsed-time deltas. Scoped per the rc7 spec author principles (tool calls and per-turn diff stats deferred; --json only format toggle; --limit lands in v1; honest-signal flag named append_turns_recorded_together not all_turns_same_second). Engine in internal/engine/trace.go (~250 LOC); CLI in internal/cli/trace.go (~140 LOC) under Daily commands group; 10 tests covering the spec acceptance cases.
summary.why: log lists intents, show explains decisions, but until now nothing showed an intent unfolding over time. Useful for: (a) post-task review when a complex Claude Code run produced a PR and the engineer wants to see what the agent actually did when, (b) reviewing a teammate PR and asking "why did this file change three times before settling", (c) future audit basis. The rc7 spec correctly observed the gap and the user pinned the scope tightly.
summary.user_goal: Make intent unfolding observable without requiring new actor-log schema or hook integration; deliver a useful v1 today, leave room for replay/tool-use/audit modes later.

decisions.point: How to handle non-existent IntentStartedEvent / IntentAppendedEvent
decisions.chose: Synthesize from local draft files for own intents; fall back to sealed_at floor + cross_actor flag for others
decisions.rationale: Spec referenced events that do not exist (start/append are local-only). Two paths: (1) extend actor log schema to include start/append events — large change with cross-actor sync implications; (2) honestly synthesize from existing local data and flag the cross-actor reduction. Picked (2) per rc7 principle no new schema. The cross_actor flag is the honest signal.

decisions.point: Honest-signal field naming: all_turns_same_second vs append_turns_recorded_together
decisions.rationale: User feedback in spec footer was sharp: start and seal are EXPECTED to have different timestamps; the diagnostic question is specifically whether append turns share the second. Renamed before shipping to lock in the precise semantic.

decisions.point: Files-touched display: paths only vs paths + diff stats
decisions.rationale: rc6 sealed fingerprint stores files_touched as []string. Adding line-level deltas would need a schema bump. v1 ships with what is available; future PR can extend the fingerprint if real users ask for the deltas.
A 50-turn intent will produce a wall of output; --limit is natural protection. Implementation cost is ~5 LOC. Per user explicit preference.
Per user. compact / detailed are aesthetics that can be added later if they prove load-bearing. One way to do it now keeps the CLI surface small.
rc7 principle of "v1 useful not pretty". Information density carries the value. If users ever say "I want it prettier", we know what to add.

rejected.alternative: Add IntentStartedEvent / IntentAppendedEvent to actor log so cross-actor traces have full detail
rejected.reason: Schema change with cross-actor sync implications; doubles event volume; out of scope for v1.
Separate scope; spec defers to v0.4. Land the static view first; learn from dogfood whether replay is actually useful.

rejected.alternative: Surface tool-use turns via the cursor/codex/claude hooks shipped in PRs #38/#41/#45
rejected.reason: Requires new turn type and hook-side schema; rc7 explicitly defers this to a future iteration.
trace is a read command; making it network-dependent for the common case (looking at your own intent) would slow the most frequent path. Stays read-local; spec agrees.

risks: Cross-actor traces show start time = seal time which is a lie about elapsed duration. Mitigated by surface-level cross_actor=true and the human-readable note. JSON callers must check the flag before computing duration.

The append_turns_recorded_together flag is computed as "all share the same RFC3339 second". A 1-second-spread batch (write at .999 then .001 of next second) would not trigger. Acceptable for v1 — the flag is informational, not load-bearing.
resolveIntentID iterates the entire view + drafts each call. For a 1000-intent repo this is O(N) per trace. Currently negligible (small repos); revisit when dogfood shows intent count > 500.

collectAllEvents is called twice per Trace if used together with auto-sync (once by sync inside auto-pin, once by Trace). Cached at the actor-log layer via PR #29 cat-file --batch so cost is small but not zero.
followups: When hooks-driven tool-use turns land (post-rc7), extend the timeline with TraceTurnToolUse + corresponding renderer.
--replay interactive mode (cobra prompt or TUI) for the v0.4 audit scope.
Add --since / --until time-range filters per rc7 spec §6 v0.2 list.
If file-level diff stats become available in fingerprint (rc8+), surface them inline in the files-touched block.
Compare-mode `mainline trace --compare <intent2>` for v0.3+ as the spec lists.

architectural_claims: Trace is a read-only timeline view derived from existing actor-log events plus local draft files; no new schema introduced.

Cross-actor intents trace cleanly via summary.cross_actor=true plus a synthetic start whose timestamp is the seal floor; per-append detail is unavailable by design until hooks instrument the cross-actor path.
append_turns_recorded_together is the honest signal that turns were batch-written; trace surfaces it but does NOT warn — the rc7 product principle is to be honest, not judgmental.
behavioral_changes: New mainline trace command in Daily commands group.
Engine.Service.Trace and Engine.Service.resolveIntentID are new public methods.
AGENTS.md template gains a show vs trace callout in the read-team-intents section.
